title: porophyllum coloratum var. coloratum
intro: Porophyllum coloratum var. coloratum, also known as 'Mexican Tea' or 'Wild Mexican Tea', is a plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to Mexico. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Porophyllum coloratum var. coloratum include relief from colds, fever, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. The plant can be consumed as a tea, infused in water, or used in herbal preparations such as tinctures and capsules, with its leaves being the primary part used for medicinal purposes.
